name: Datadog
one_liner: Cloud observability and monitoring platform (metrics, traces, logs, dashboards, monitors, incidents) with a comprehensive HTTP REST API.
access_notes: Datadog offers both a free 14-day trial (no credit card required) and a free tier. Signing up gives immediate access to Organization Settings where you generate an API key (DD-API-KEY) and application key (DD-APPLICATION-KEY) with no human approval. Both keys are needed for most read operations; the API key alone suffices for many write/submit operations.
api_notes: Documented HTTP REST API returning JSON with resource-oriented URLs and standard status codes. Covers a very large surface: metrics, logs, events, dashboards, monitors, downtimes, incidents, SLOs, synthetics, RUM, security monitoring, hosts, users/roles, key management, service accounts, and more — well over 100 operations. Official client libraries for Java, Python, Ruby, Go, JavaScript, and Rust. Auth via DD-API-KEY (+ DD-APPLICATION-KEY) headers. Site-specific base URLs (US1, EU, etc.).
mcp_notes: Datadog ships an official, vendor-managed remote MCP server (docs.datadoghq.com/mcp_server, repo github.com/datadog-labs/mcp-server). Endpoint https://mcp.datadoghq.com/api/unstable/mcp-server/mcp (EU: mcp.datadoghq.eu). Provides access to APM, logs, metrics, monitors, dashboards, security signals, incidents; distributed as an Anthropic plugin. HIPAA-eligible (not GovCloud compatible).
main_blocker: None significant. Dual-key auth (API key + application key) is slightly more involved than a single token, and application key secrets cannot be retrieved after creation, but self-serve credentials are available instantly on a free/trial account.

evidence.claim: API uses DD-API-KEY header (and DD-APPLICATION-KEY for some endpoints); reads require an application key, writes require an API key.
The Datadog API is an HTTP REST API using resource-oriented URLs, returning JSON and using standard HTTP status codes.
Datadog offers a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.
Datadog ships an official managed remote MCP server for querying observability data from AI agents.
Application keys are created under Organization Settings > Application Keys and the secret cannot be retrieved after creation.

open_questions: Exact API rate limits and whether the free tier (vs. trial) has any endpoint restrictions were not verified in detail.
